subject: Re: S76 Divan Arm Rests
body:
leave as is

--- LVJET@aol.com wrote:

> Jeffrey,
> According to Sikorsky, seatbelts for only 3 can be
> certified if 2"  armrests 
> are installed on each side. Photos below are C++
> mock up with  armrests. 
> currently we are installing bench seating with 4
> seatbelts.
> Lv
> > From: "Basile, Carmelo S         SIK"
> <CBasile@SIKORSKY.COM>
> To: "Larry Visoski (E-mail)" <lvjet@aol.com>
> CC: "McTaggart, Thomas S       SIK"
> <TMcTaggart@SIKORSKY.COM>
> Subject: Divan Arm Rests
> Date: Fri, 25 Aug 2006 14:30:21 -0400
> 
> Larry,
> 
> Attached are a few photos of the 3-place divan
> displayed in the "Silencer"
> mockup. The 2-inch outboard arm rests that you see
> are unusual as they limit
> the capacity of the divan to three-passengers rather
> than the usual 4. The
> consequence would be the same if outboard arm rests
> were to be installed
> into the aft forward-facing divan. The with of the
> aircraft is essentially
> the same at these locations and the results would be
> the same. I hope these
> help you.
> 
> Please get back to me regarding the approvals of the
> documents, materials
> and the exterior as per my 8/3/06 e-mail.
> 
>  <<DSC00043.JPG>>  <<DSC00047.JPG>> 
> <<DSC00062.JPG>> 
> 
> Thank you.
> 
> 
> Mr. Carmen Basile
> Senior Interior Designer
> Air Vehicle Design
> Sikorsky Aircraft

body_html:
leave as is

--- LVJET@aol.com wrote:

Jeffrey,
According to Sikorsky, seatbelts for only 3 can be
certified if 2" armrests
are installed on each side. Photos below are C++
mock up with armrests.
currently we are installing bench seating with 4
seatbelts.
Lv

From: "Basile, Carmelo S SIK"
CBasile@SIKORSKY.COM
To: "Larry Visoski (E-mail)" lvjet@aol.com
CC: "McTaggart, Thomas S SIK"
TMcTaggart@SIKORSKY.COM
Subject: Divan Arm Rests
Date: Fri, 25 Aug 2006 14:30:21 -0400

Larry,

Attached are a few photos of the 3-place divan
displayed in the "Silencer"
mockup. The 2-inch outboard arm rests that you see
are unusual as they limit
the capacity of the divan to three-passengers rather
than the usual 4. The
consequence would be the same if outboard arm rests
were to be installed
into the aft forward-facing divan. The with of the
aircraft is essentially
the same at these locations and the results would be
the same. I hope these
help you.

Please get back to me regarding the approvals of the
documents, materials
and the exterior as per my 8/3/06 e-mail.

<<DSC00043.JPG>> <<DSC00047.JPG>>
<<DSC00062.JPG>>

Thank you.

Mr. Carmen Basile
Senior Interior Designer
Air Vehicle Design
Sikorsky Aircraft
6900 Main Street
Mail Stop S313A
Stratford, CT 06615-9129
203-386-3754 (O)
860-998-6497 (F)
cbasile@sikorsky.com
